authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC staff's fee-rule compliance guide is the authoritative federal consumer-protection baseline for live-event ticket total-price disclosure, mandatory-fee treatment, truthful refundability representations, secondary marketplace pricing responsibilities, and state-law conflict handling.

authoritative_sources.title: 15 U.S.C. 45c - Unfair and deceptive acts and practices relating to circumvention of ticket access control measures
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 15 U.S.C. 45c is the controlling federal statute for ticket-access control circumvention, fake-identity purchasing, and resale of tickets obtained in violation of posted security or purchasing rules.

authoritative_sources.title: BOTS Act compliance: Time for a refresher?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC enforcement guidance is authoritative for how the agency currently frames covered events, prohibited conduct, fake-identity and purchase-limit circumvention examples, and enforcement posture for ticket sellers and resellers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Anti-bot evidence review and seller or reseller enforcement posture
used_for: ticket seller and reseller evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: ADA Requirements: Ticket Sales
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice, Civil Rights Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ ADA guidance is authoritative for assigned-seat entertainment ticket workflows involving accessible-seat sales, comparable pricing, companion seats, hold and release rules, secondary-market exchanges, fraud-prevention attestations, and staff-training expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Booking, accessible-seat, and secondary-market control review

authoritative_sources.title: Crowd Management Safety Guidelines for Retailers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA crowd-management guidance is an authoritative public-event operations reference for entry controls, occupancy limits, emergency planning, signage, accessible ingress, and coordination with police, fire, and medical responders when crowd risk is present.

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Resiliency Guide: Public Assembly Annex
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA is the federal security and resilience lead for critical and public-facing infrastructure, and its public-assembly annex is authoritative for venue stakeholders, security coordination, suspicious-activity handling, ingress and egress controls, and venue-security escalation posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Public-venue security coordination and suspicious-incident escalation

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm event, venue, channel, jurisdiction, seller versus reseller role, event status, posted policy version, and adjacent approval owners before any compliance conclusion is produced.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map entertainment obligations to fee-disclosure controls, accessible-seat handling, booking and transfer rules, refund-policy posture, anti-bot controls, and crowd-safety escalation expectations for the exact event and sales channel under review.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and system-of-record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate whether order, payment, seat, accessible-inventory, refund, transfer, complaint, fraud, and incident artifacts exist in the correct systems of record with traceable ownership and policy versioning.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: High-volume exception handling and customer-impact triage
workflow_stages.description: Convert onsale spikes, reschedule waves, booking mismatches, refund requests, and accommodation issues into explicit blocked, routed, or review-ready states without skipping prerequisite checks.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Boundary, remediation, and escalation
workflow_stages.description: Refuse legal interpretation, pricing overrides, refund exceptions, fraud findings, and safety signoff while routing the issue to the correct authorized owner with preserved evidence context.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: ticket orders, reservation confirmations, invoices, and payment tender records
fee-disclosure screenshots or logs, price cards, promo records, posted customer notices, and policy-version evidence
seat maps, accessible-seat inventory, companion-seat arrangements, hold and release logs, and accommodation notes
transfer records, secondary-market exchange notes, ticket reissue or cancellation records, and refund or service-recovery records
guest complaint tickets, call or chat transcripts, chargeback notes, and escalation summaries
bot-mitigation alerts, account-access logs, suspicious-resale signals, and fraud or loss-prevention case notes
incident reports, crowd-management plans, occupancy notes, ingress and egress procedures, and security escalation logs
staff-training materials, knowledge-base articles, and audit or review checklists
systems_of_record: incident management or security log system

allowed: Classify entertainment compliance requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required before safe compliance handling can proceed.
Map ticketing, pricing, accessible-seat, anti-bot, and venue-safety obligations to named evidence artifacts, owners, and review cadence.
Prepare evidence-backed control matrices, readiness summaries, remediation notes, audit-prep packets, and next-step packages without claiming pricing, refund, fraud, or safety finality.
escalate: Required order, payment, accessible-seat, fraud, incident, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, chargeback adjudication, fraud disposition, legal interpretation, or venue-security authority.
State or local law, promoter terms, venue policy, and the public baseline materially conflict.
A crowd-safety issue, blocked accessible entrance, suspicious bot activity, or high-volume guest impact changes whether ordinary ticketing handling may proceed.
refuse: Approve price changes, fee waivers, refunds, comps, or policy exceptions without documented authority.
Provide legal advice, formal ADA or consumer-protection signoff, or regulator-response conclusions.
Certify fraud absence, clear suspicious resale, or declare a safety incident resolved without the required authorized owner.

domain_failure_modes: advertising or communicating ticket price, fees, or refundability without tying the answer to current total-price evidence and policy version
treating accessible-seat obligations as generic customer service instead of same-channel, same-stage, hold and release, transfer, and fraud-aware ticket controls
ignoring bot-circumvention, fake-identity, or suspicious-resale evidence and allowing the case to look like normal guest-service work
collapsing a crowd-management or blocked-accessibility incident into an ordinary refund or service-recovery workflow
allowing high-volume onsale or reschedule pressure to skip booking, payment, accessible-seat, or incident evidence checks
guessing through jurisdiction-specific ticketing or refund-law differences instead of recording the conflict and escalating
overgeneralizing the entertainment label and answering pricing, guest-recovery, fraud, or operations decisions as if the lane owned those authorities

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State and local ticket refund, resale, and fee-disclosure variations
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The validated baseline is anchored in FTC, DOJ, OSHA, CISA, and federal law. Jurisdiction-specific ticket resale, refund, and disclosure rules still vary, so runtime must retrieve jurisdiction and seller versus reseller posture before the lane closes case-specific answers.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Promoter, venue, and marketplace rule overlays
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources define the minimum ticketing, accessibility, anti-bot, and safety baseline, but each tenant still needs current promoter contracts, venue house rules, marketplace terms, and incident-command routing before the lane can close exception-heavy live-event work with full specificity.

notes: Authoritative order, booking, pricing, accessible-seat, refund, fraud, and incident records are the ticketing, payment, CRM, fraud, incident, and policy systems designated by the tenant, constrained by the federal consumer-protection, accessibility, and safety baseline.

The lane stops before final price setting, refund authorization, guest-recovery discretion, fraud adjudication, venue-security command, legal interpretation, and regulator-facing complaint response even when customer volume, sellout pressure, or event-day urgency is high.
